questions.q: Who were Abhimanyu's parents?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu was the son of Arjuna and Subhadra, Krishna's sister.

questions.q: What relation was Krishna to Abhimanyu?
questions.explanation: As the brother of Subhadra, Krishna was Abhimanyu's maternal uncle.

questions.q: Inside which battle formation was Abhimanyu trapped and killed?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu died inside the Chakravyuha, the wheel formation arrayed by Drona.

questions.q: Whom did Abhimanyu marry?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu wed Uttara, daughter of King Virata, after the Pandavas' year in disguise.

questions.q: Who was Abhimanyu's famous son?
questions.explanation: Parikshit, born after Abhimanyu's death, carried the Kuru line forward.

questions.q: At roughly what age is Abhimanyu traditionally said to have died?
questions.explanation: Tradition remembers Abhimanyu as a sixteen-year-old at his death in the war.

questions.q: Where is Abhimanyu traditionally said to have learned to pierce the Chakravyuha?
options: In a dream sent by Shiva
questions.explanation: The traditional account relates that he heard Arjuna explain the entry to Subhadra while still in her womb, learning the way in but never the way out.

questions.q: On which day of the Kurukshetra War did Abhimanyu fall?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu was slain on the thirteenth day, while Arjuna was lured away from the main front.

questions.q: Which side did Abhimanyu fight for in the war?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu was one of the foremost young warriors of the Pandava army.

questions.q: Who commanded the Kaurava army when the Chakravyuha was formed?
questions.explanation: Drona, then commander-in-chief, arrayed the Chakravyuha on the thirteenth day.

questions.q: What was the fatal gap in Abhimanyu's knowledge of the Chakravyuha?
options: He never learned how to exit it
He could not break its innermost ring
He knew only its night-time form
He could enter it only on foot
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu knew how to pierce inward but not the way out, leaving him trapped alone.

questions.q: Why could the Pandava warriors not follow Abhimanyu into the formation?
options: Jayadratha held the breach against them
Their chariots were mired in mud
Krishna ordered them to hold back
questions.explanation: Jayadratha, empowered by a boon from Shiva, single-handedly blocked the Pandavas at the breach.

questions.q: Whose boon allowed Jayadratha to hold off the Pandava brothers that day?
questions.explanation: Shiva had granted Jayadratha the power to check the Pandavas, except Arjuna, for one day.

questions.q: What vow did Arjuna take on hearing of his son's death?
options: To kill Jayadratha before the next sunset
To slay Drona within three days
To never use the Gandiva again
To end the war by single combat
questions.explanation: Arjuna swore to kill Jayadratha by sunset the next day or enter the fire himself.

questions.q: Which Kaurava prince, Duryodhana's son, did Abhimanyu slay in the formation?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu killed Lakshmana, enraging Duryodhana and sealing the resolve to destroy him.

questions.q: Who cut Abhimanyu's bow from behind during the final assault?
questions.explanation: On Drona's counsel, Karna severed Abhimanyu's bow from behind, a breach of fair combat.

questions.q: Deprived of weapons, what did Abhimanyu finally fight with?
questions.explanation: Disarmed, Abhimanyu lifted a chariot wheel and fought on with wheel and mace until the end.

questions.q: Who struck the final blow that killed Abhimanyu?
options: The son of Dushasana, in a mace duel
questions.explanation: In the mace duel both fighters fell stunned; Dushasana's son rose first and struck the rising, exhausted Abhimanyu a fatal blow with his mace.

questions.q: How many great Kaurava warriors surrounded Abhimanyu at once, against the rules of war?
questions.explanation: Six maharathis — including Drona, Karna, Kripa and Ashwatthama — attacked the lone youth together.

questions.q: What was Uttara's condition when Abhimanyu died?
options: She was held hostage in Hastinapura
questions.explanation: Uttara carried their unborn son Parikshit, the future heir of the Kuru line.

questions.q: Who revived Abhimanyu's stillborn son after Ashwatthama's astra struck him in the womb?
questions.explanation: Krishna restored the child Parikshit to life, fulfilling his promise to Uttara and the Pandavas.

questions.q: During the Pandavas' thirteenth year of exile, where did Abhimanyu live?
options: In Dwarka with his mother's kin
In the Kamyaka forest with Draupadi
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu grew up among the Yadavas in Dwarka while his father lived in exile.

questions.q: Abhimanyu is described as an incarnation of which celestial being?
options: Varchas, son of the moon god Soma
questions.explanation: The epic names Abhimanyu as the incarnation of Varchas, son of Soma.

questions.q: What condition did Soma set when allowing Varchas to be born as Abhimanyu?
options: He would never father a child
He would return to Soma after sixteen years
He could not be defeated before Arjuna's eyes
questions.explanation: Soma lent his son to the world for only sixteen years, foreshadowing Abhimanyu's early death.

questions.q: Which king of Kosala fell to Abhimanyu inside the Chakravyuha?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu slew Brihadbala, the Kosala king fighting for the Kauravas.

questions.q: What does the name 'Abhimanyu' essentially convey?
questions.explanation: The name suggests one of fiery valor and proud wrath, fitting his battlefield fury.

questions.q: In some southern and folk traditions, Abhimanyu also weds which cousin, in a tale absent from the Sanskrit epic?
questions.explanation: The Vatsala or Sasirekha romance, with Ghatotkacha's help, is a beloved folk addition, not part of Vyasa's text.

questions.q: Which teacher gave Abhimanyu his formal training in arms?
options: Arjuna and the Vrishni heroes like Pradyumna
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu trained under his father and the Yadava warriors during his Dwarka upbringing.

questions.q: Why did Yudhishthira sanction sending the boy Abhimanyu into the Chakravyuha?
options: Only Abhimanyu among those present could pierce it
Abhimanyu demanded the honor by oath
Drona had challenged him by name
questions.explanation: With Arjuna lured away by the Samsaptakas, no one else in the army knew how to break the wheel's entry.

questions.q: What consequence within the war directly followed Abhimanyu's death?
options: Drona resigned his command in shame
Arjuna slew Jayadratha at the false sunset
Bhima razed the Kaurava camp that night
Krishna lifted his vow and took up arms
questions.explanation: Arjuna's vow culminated on the fourteenth day, when he beheaded Jayadratha as the sun seemed to set — hidden, in the famous telling, by Krishna's illusion.
